summary: Validated the localStorage Bearer-token auth migration end-to-end via the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 8 critical flows pass: super_admin login → /super (super-title rendered), super_admin → /admin (admin-page + admin-tab-radio rendered, NOT blank), super_admin → /dj (dj-studio + dj-studio-title rendered, NOT blank), hard reload of /super and /admin both keep the user logged in (cached via rl_user/rl_access_token in localStorage), logout clears both localStorage keys, admin@radiolatina.fm/admin123 redirects to /admin, dj@radiolatina.fm/dj123 redirects to /dj. Login API (POST /api/auth/login) returns 200 with access_token and user (with role) in body — frontend correctly persists into localStorage('rl_access_token') and localStorage('rl_user'). The previously reported blank-page bug on /admin and /dj for super_admin is RESOLVED.

issues: Carry-over from iter 2-4: console warning '' still emitted (empty image src for entities without photo/banner). Non-blocking.

action_items: (LOW carry-over) Replace empty with conditional rendering when photo_path/banner_path is empty to silence the console error.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.refresh() trusts cached user with no server validation — if the JWT is server-rotated/expired, the user sees a stale UI until the next API call returns 401. Acceptable trade-off given the ingress CORS issue, but consider a lightweight /api/auth/me ping (best-effort, no spinner) on app boot to invalidate cached sessions earlier.

axios response interceptor only clears localStorage on 401; it does NOT call setUser(null), so the Auth context still shows the cached user until the next route change / refresh() call. Consider exposing a logout() callback from api.js or dispatching a custom event so AuthContext can react immediately to forced-logout.

Token is stored in localStorage (XSS-readable). The original cookie design was httpOnly and safer; the workaround is justified by the ingress 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' bug, but document this trade-off and ensure all user-rendered content is properly escaped.

Login.jsx redirect default falls through to navigate('/admin') for any non-super_admin/non-dj role — fine today (only 3 roles), but a future 'viewer' role would be silently routed into the admin dashboard; prefer an explicit role match with a guarded fallback to '/'.
test_credentials: Used credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md: pzsuave007@gmail.com/MXmedia007 (super_admin), admin@radiolatina.fm/admin123 (admin), dj@radiolatina.fm/dj123 (dj).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth is now Bearer-token in localStorage (rl_access_token + rl_user JSON). Verified flows: super→/super, super→/admin, super→/dj, hard-reload persistence on /super and /admin, logout clears storage, admin→/admin, dj→/dj. Login intercept observed POST /api/auth/login returning 200 with both access_token and user. The previously suspected 'page.click on submit didn't fire /api/auth/login' issue did NOT reproduce in this run — the standard form submit + Bearer interceptor work correctly. Watch for: (a) console error '' (non-blocking), (b) AuthContext does not auto-clear React state on response-interceptor 401; only localStorage is cleared until next route change.
rca of the issue: Original blank-page bug on /admin and /dj for super_admin was caused by the Kubernetes ingress injecting 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' which forced the browser to silently drop httpOnly cookies (incompatible with credentials:true). With cookies dropped, /api/auth/me returned 401 → AuthContext set user=null → role-gated pages rendered nothing (blank). Fix verified: frontend now reads access_token from login response body, stores it + user in localStorage, and axios attaches Authorization: Bearer on every request — fully bypassing the ingress cookie problem.
